old_column.content: * A React hook that automatically triggers fetches of data from an endpoint, 'subscribes' the component to the cached data, and reads the request status and cached data from the Redux store. The component will re-render as the loading status changes and the data becomes available.

new_column.content: * A React hook that automatically triggers fetches of data from an endpoint, 'subscribes' the component to the cached data, and reads the request status and cached data from the Redux store. The component will re-render as the loading status changes and the data becomes available.

old_column.content: * The query arg is used as a cache key. Changing the query arg will tell the hook to re-fetch the data if it does not exist in the cache already, and the hook will return the data for that query arg once it's available.

new_column.content: * The query arg is used as a cache key. Changing the query arg will tell the hook to re-fetch the data if it does not exist in the cache already, and the hook will return the data for that query arg once it's available.

old_column.content: * This hook combines the functionality of both [`useQueryState`](#usequerystate) and [`useQuerySubscription`](#usequerysubscription) together, and is intended to be used in the majority of situations.

new_column.content: * This hook combines the functionality of both [`useQueryState`](#usequerystate) and [`useQuerySubscription`](#usequerysubscription) together, and is intended to be used in the majority of situations.

old_column.content: * Defaults to `false`. This setting allows you to control whether if a cached result is already available, RTK Query will only serve a cached result, or if it should `refetch` when set to `true` or if an adequate amount of time has passed since the last successful query result.

new_column.content: * Defaults to `false`. This setting allows you to control whether if a cached result is already available, RTK Query will only serve a cached result, or if it should `refetch` when set to `true` or if an adequate amount of time has passed since the last successful query result.

old_column.content: * - `false` - Will not cause a query to be performed _unless_ it does not exist yet.

new_column.content: * - `false` - Will not cause a query to be performed _unless_ it does not exist yet.

old_column.content: * - `true` - Will always refetch when a new subscriber to a query is added. Behaves the same as calling the `refetch` callback or passing `forceRefetch: true` in the action creator.

new_column.content: * - `true` - Will always refetch when a new subscriber to a query is added. Behaves the same as calling the `refetch` callback or passing `forceRefetch: true` in the action creator.

old_column.content: * - `number` - **Value is in seconds**. If a number is provided and there is an existing query in the cache, it will compare the current time vs the last fulfilled timestamp, and only refetch if enough time has elapsed.

new_column.content: * - `number` - **Value is in seconds**. If a number is provided and there is an existing query in the cache, it will compare the current time vs the last fulfilled timestamp, and only refetch if enough time has elapsed.

old_column.content: * If you specify this option alongside `skip: true`, this **will not be evaluated** until `skip` is false.

new_column.content: * If you specify this option alongside `skip: true`, this **will not be evaluated** until `skip` is false.

* {@linkcode UseQuerySubscriptionOptions} does not depend on a specific query

new_column.content: * - `false` - Will not cause a query to be performed _unless_ it does not exist yet.

old_column.content: * - `true` - Will always refetch when a new subscriber to a query is added. Behaves the same as calling the `refetch` callback or passing `forceRefetch: true` in the action creator.

new_column.content: * - `true` - Will always refetch when a new subscriber to a query is added. Behaves the same as calling the `refetch` callback or passing `forceRefetch: true` in the action creator.

old_column.content: * - `number` - **Value is in seconds**. If a number is provided and there is an existing query in the cache, it will compare the current time vs the last fulfilled timestamp, and only refetch if enough time has elapsed.

new_column.content: * - `number` - **Value is in seconds**. If a number is provided and there is an existing query in the cache, it will compare the current time vs the last fulfilled timestamp, and only refetch if enough time has elapsed.

old_column.content: * If you specify this option alongside `skip: true`, this **will not be evaluated** until `skip` is false.

new_column.content: * If you specify this option alongside `skip: true`, this **will not be evaluated** until `skip` is false.

old_column.content: * A React hook that automatically triggers fetches of data from an endpoint, 'subscribes' the component to the cached data, and reads the request status and cached data from the Redux store. The component will re-render as the loading status changes and the data becomes available.  Additionally, it will cache multiple "pages" worth of responses within a single cache entry, and allows fetching more pages forwards and backwards from the current cached pages.

new_column.content: * A React hook that automatically triggers fetches of data from an endpoint, 'subscribes' the component to the cached data, and reads the request status and cached data from the Redux store. The component will re-render as the loading status changes and the data becomes available.  Additionally, it will cache multiple "pages" worth of responses within a single cache entry, and allows fetching more pages forwards and backwards from the current cached pages.

old_column.content: * The query arg is used as a cache key. Changing the query arg will tell the hook to re-fetch the data if it does not exist in the cache already, and the hook will return the data for that query arg once it's available.

new_column.content: * The query arg is used as a cache key. Changing the query arg will tell the hook to re-fetch the data if it does not exist in the cache already, and the hook will return the data for that query arg once it's available.

old_column.content: *  The `data` field will be a `{pages: Data[], pageParams: PageParam[]}` structure containing all fetched page responses and the corresponding page param values for each page. You may use this to render individual pages, combine all pages into a single infinite list, or other display logic as needed.

new_column.content: *  The `data` field will be a `{pages: Data[], pageParams: PageParam[]}` structure containing all fetched page responses and the corresponding page param values for each page. You may use this to render individual pages, combine all pages into a single infinite list, or other display logic as needed.

old_column.content: * This hook combines the functionality of both [`useInfiniteQueryState`](#useinfinitequerystate) and [`useInfiniteQuerySubscription`](#useinfinitequerysubscription) together, and is intended to be used in the majority of situations.

new_column.content: * This hook combines the functionality of both [`useInfiniteQueryState`](#useinfinitequerystate) and [`useInfiniteQuerySubscription`](#useinfinitequerysubscription) together, and is intended to be used in the majority of situations.

old_column.content: * As with normal query hooks, `skipToken` is a valid argument that will skip the query from executing.

new_column.content: * As with normal query hooks, `skipToken` is a valid argument that will skip the query from executing.

old_column.content: * By default, the initial request will use the `initialPageParam` value that was defined on the infinite query endpoint. If you want to start from a different value, you can pass `initialPageParam` as part of the hook options to override that initial request value.

new_column.content: * By default, the initial request will use the `initialPageParam` value that was defined on the infinite query endpoint. If you want to start from a different value, you can pass `initialPageParam` as part of the hook options to override that initial request value.

old_column.content: * Use the returned `fetchNextPage` and `fetchPreviousPage` methods on the hook result object to trigger fetches forwards and backwards. These will always calculate the next or previous page param based on the current cached pages and the provided `getNext/PreviousPageParam` callbacks defined in the endpoint.

new_column.content: * Use the returned `fetchNextPage` and `fetchPreviousPage` methods on the hook result object to trigger fetches forwards and backwards. These will always calculate the next or previous page param based on the current cached pages and the provided `getNext/PreviousPageParam` callbacks defined in the endpoint.

new_column.content: * - Automatically triggers requests to retrieve data based on the hook argument and whether cached data exists by default
